domains.title: E-Mobility & Automotive
domains.description: High-voltage power systems, battery management, and automotive safety validation

projects.title: Firmware Flashing Automation Tool
projects.tagline: Production GUI tool enabling global EV firmware updates
projects.cardOutcome: Replaced multiple CLI-based workflows with one GUI — field technicians now flash firmware without engineering support.
projects.situation: Firmware updates required CLI expertise and direct engineering support. Updating multiple embedded controllers had no unified interface, making the process error-prone for manufacturing, validation, and field teams.
projects.task: Create a production-grade flashing tool that abstracts firmware update complexity, prevents configuration errors, and enables non-engineers to perform updates independently.
projects.action: Built a Qt/C++ GUI from the ground up with CAN-based diagnostic workflows, configuration-driven flashing logic, real-time logging, and error-proofing. Incorporated cross-site feedback to standardize usability across U.S. and Poland teams.
result: Non-engineers now perform firmware updates independently
Standardized workflows across global manufacturing and field teams
Freed firmware engineers from routine update support
Improved manufacturing and service readiness for EV platform

cardMetrics.label: EFFICIENCY
cardMetrics.value: Reduced flashing steps by 30-50%

cardMetrics.label: SCALE
cardMetrics.value: Deployed to U.S. and Poland teams

cardMetrics.label: RELIABILITY
cardMetrics.value: Eliminated manual flashing errors

projects.title: Hardware-in-Loop (HiL) Validation – Enterprise EV Power Systems
projects.tagline: Hardware-in-Loop validation for a new OEM customer pursuit
projects.cardOutcome: Resolved setup blockers stalling a new OEM program — 40+ fault types verified and regression suite running.
projects.situation: A new OEM program required firmware changes beyond an existing baseline. Global teams were blocked by test setup issues, stalling regression testing and preventing automated HiL execution.
projects.task: Execute regression and requirements-based HiL test suites, validate safety diagnostics and fault handling, and remove setup blockers impacting distributed engineering teams.
projects.action: Ran Python/pytest HiL test suites, configured communication interfaces, injected hardware-level faults across 40+ conditions, and resolved preconditions blocking remote teams from long-running automated tests.
result: Firmware releases proceeded with confidence for OEM program
Confirmed reliable fault logging and diagnostic coverage
Unblocked globally distributed teams from stalled test execution
Improved HiL infrastructure readiness for future programs

cardMetrics.label: QUALITY
cardMetrics.value: Validated 40+ fault types

cardMetrics.label: SAFETY
cardMetrics.value: Verified critical safety diagnostics for EV program

cardMetrics.label: UNBLOCKED
cardMetrics.value: Removed setup blockers for global engineering teams

projects.title: Battery Management System Validation (isoSPI)
projects.tagline: isoSPI validation for high-voltage battery monitoring
projects.cardOutcome: Turned a blocking isoSPI ambiguity into a repeatable workflow — engineers now validate independently without SME support.
projects.situation: No reproducible workflow existed for validating isoSPI communication in a safety-critical battery management context. Engineers were blocked by unclear sequencing and memory interactions, creating SME dependency.
projects.task: Develop and document a reliable isoSPI validation flow that enables independent execution, confirms safety-critical communication paths, and removes configuration ambiguity.
projects.action: Validated isoSPI communication between host controller and battery monitoring IC hardware. Verified memory access, GPIO behavior, and protection-related configurations. Created reproducible engineering documentation and served as cross-team technical reference.
result: Unblocked battery system development stalled on isoSPI issues
Validation workflows became repeatable and self-serve
Improved knowledge sharing and consistency across teams
Tooling and version ambiguities resolved end-to-end

cardMetrics.label: UNBLOCKED
cardMetrics.value: Resolved isoSPI blockers across eMobility team

cardMetrics.label: SAFETY
cardMetrics.value: Validated safety-critical HV battery communication paths

cardMetrics.label: KNOWLEDGE
cardMetrics.value: Created reproducible procedures — eliminated test ambiguity

domains.title: IoT & Smart Home
domains.description: Connected devices, wireless protocols, and smart home ecosystem integration

projects.title: Matter & Thread Protocol Evaluation
projects.tagline: Evaluating the next innovation beyond Wi-Fi for Eaton's smart home roadmap
projects.cardOutcome: Replaced speculation with a working Matter ecosystem — concrete adoption guidance delivered to 5 teams.
projects.situation: The smart-home portfolio was Wi-Fi-only while competitors shipped Matter devices. Teams lacked hands-on experience to evaluate adoption feasibility and interoperability risks.
projects.task: Build a working Matter + Thread ecosystem, compare behavior across ecosystems and device types, and identify platform-level considerations for adoption.
projects.action: Built a functional test ecosystem with industry reference hardware. Commissioned devices across multiple ecosystems, compared Wi-Fi vs. Thread characteristics, evaluated interoperability and security, and benchmarked against competitors. Synthesized findings for 5 cross-functional teams.
result: Enabled alignment across product, firmware, platform, and cybersecurity teams
Replaced assumptions with validated hands-on technical data
Created a shared technical baseline across stakeholders
Accelerated decision-making on next-generation connectivity

cardMetrics.label: STRATEGY
cardMetrics.value: Directly informed wiring device product roadmap

cardMetrics.label: BREADTH
cardMetrics.value: Findings shared with 5 cross-functional teams

cardMetrics.label: COMPETITIVE
cardMetrics.value: Benchmarked against Eve, Leviton, ELEGRP

projects.complianceNote: All internal analyses, platform evaluations, and roadmap-related insights remain the intellectual property of Eaton. This project is presented externally only at a high-level, non-confidential abstraction, consistent with Eaton's confidentiality and ethics guidance.

links.label: Presentations / Findings (Internal)
links.note: Available to Eaton employees with appropriate access

projects.title: Smart WiFi Devices Testing
projects.tagline: End-to-end qualification of 50+ smart home devices before product launch
projects.cardOutcome: Caught firmware defects across ~50 devices before launch — no prior test plan existed.
projects.situation: A new firmware release changed provisioning, OTA, and app interoperability. No test plan covered these changes across the full smart-device portfolio, creating launch risk.
projects.task: Create a comprehensive test strategy, execute qualification testing across all device categories, and ensure app and voice-assistant interoperability before release.
projects.action: Built and executed test plans covering BLE provisioning, Wi-Fi onboarding, OTA updates, mobile app interop, and voice-assistant integration. Used vendor tooling and RTOS-level debugging. Logged defects and worked with firmware engineers to resolve before release.
result: Prevented firmware defects from reaching customers
Enabled a clean product launch across the connected-home line
Validated device-to-app and voice-assistant interoperability at scale
Built the team's first structured qualification process for this product line

cardMetrics.label: SCALE
cardMetrics.value: Qualified ~50 devices across product line

cardMetrics.label: QUALITY
cardMetrics.value: Caught firmware defects before product release

cardMetrics.label: COVERAGE
cardMetrics.value: Full BLE, OTA, App, and voice control validation

projects.title: Remote Diagnostic System for Field Fault Analysis
projects.tagline: Remote diagnostics for a device class with zero prior fault visibility
projects.cardOutcome: First time this device class had remote fault visibility — field engineers can now triage without a site visit.
projects.situation: A device class behind ~15% of field issues required costly on-site visits for every incident. Existing tools only captured post-failure states — not the signal behavior leading up to faults.
projects.task: Build a diagnostic capability that captures real-time pre-fault signal behavior, enables remote triage, and reduces dependency on on-site visits.
projects.action: Developed an embedded prototype combining STM32 data acquisition with a Raspberry Pi edge node. Implemented real-time signal capture, streaming to dashboards, and automated CSV/JSON export for engineering workflows.
result: Eliminated recurring on-site diagnostic visits for this device class
Engineering teams can now analyze pre-fault behavior remotely
Addressed the core diagnostic gap behind recurring field issues
Prototype validated with live signal monitoring and structured data export

cardMetrics.label: COST
cardMetrics.value: Reduced recurring $3K/visit on-site costs

cardMetrics.label: VISIBILITY
cardMetrics.value: Enabled real-time remote fault capture — first time for this device

cardMetrics.label: COVERAGE
cardMetrics.value: Targeted a device class behind ~15% of field service calls

domains.title: Firmware Infrastructure & Tooling
domains.description: Build systems, simulation platforms, and modern development workflows

projects.title: Firmware Simulation & Test Infrastructure
projects.tagline: What if firmware teams never needed a prototype board?
projects.cardOutcome: 4 teams now validate firmware without hardware access — lab debugging time reduced from hours to near-zero per cycle.
projects.situation: Embedded teams relied on physical prototype boards for all firmware validation. No software-in-the-loop testing existed, making every change dependent on hardware availability and slowing development.
projects.task: Enable firmware validation without physical hardware by simulating embedded platforms, supporting automated test execution, and integrating simulation into CI workflows.
projects.action: Developed simulation configurations to emulate firmware behavior. Resolved simulation gaps blocking accuracy, integrated automated test hooks for CI-driven validation, and drove adoption across four teams.
result: Eliminated prototype board dependency for routine validation
Improved firmware quality by shifting validation earlier in the cycle
Teams independently adopted simulation without ongoing support
Enabled parallel, repeatable test execution across products

cardMetrics.label: COST
cardMetrics.value: $5-7K annual savings

cardMetrics.label: SPEED
cardMetrics.value: 2x-5x faster test cycles

cardMetrics.label: SCALE
cardMetrics.value: Adopted by 4 embedded teams

projects.title: Memory-Safe Embedded Firmware Enablement (Rust)
projects.tagline: Can Rust work for safety-critical embedded firmware at Eaton?
projects.cardOutcome: First Rust firmware running on Eaton STM32 hardware — memory-safe embedded development proven viable for future projects.
projects.situation: Embedded teams used C/C++ exclusively and wanted to evaluate memory-safe alternatives. No internal Rust examples, mixed-language patterns, or onboarding paths existed.
projects.task: Prototype Rust firmware on embedded hardware, explore C/C++ interoperability, and create reusable onboarding and CI foundations for team adoption.
projects.action: Built Rust prototypes on real hardware, demonstrated C/C++ FFI integration patterns, created a starter repo with examples and setup guidance, implemented CI workflows with unit testing, and delivered internal Rust training content.
result: Mixed-language C/C++ + Rust feasibility demonstrated
Self-serve enablement materials reduced SME dependency
Reusable CI and testing patterns available for future projects
Positioned memory-safe development as a viable path forward

cardMetrics.label: ONBOARDING
cardMetrics.value: Structured docs and starter repo reduced ramp-up friction

cardMetrics.label: INNOVATION
cardMetrics.value: First working Rust firmware on Eaton STM32 hardware

cardMetrics.label: SAFETY
cardMetrics.value: Established memory-safe path for future embedded projects

domains.title: Academic & Research
domains.description: Coursework projects and published research from Georgia Tech and SRM University

projects.title: ENS160 Air Quality Sensor Driver for mbed
projects.tagline: Open-source I2C driver contributed back to the mbed community
projects.cardOutcome: Published a working I2C driver library to the mbed community — open-source contribution from coursework.
projects.situation: The SparkFun ENS160 sensor was newly launched with no C++ driver for mbed. The team wanted to contribute back to the community that supported their coursework.
projects.task: Write a C++ I2C driver for the ENS160 on mbed LPC1768, build a demo application, and publish to the mbed repository.
projects.action: Wrote a register-level I2C driver for air quality reads and sensor configuration. Built a demo with uLCD display and pushbutton input, published both to mbed, and prototyped a Pi Pico port.
result: Reusable driver library available for the mbed ecosystem
Demo application validated with live sensor reads on uLCD
Pi Pico port developed but not fully verified due to SDK limitations
Full development lifecycle: datasheet study to published, tested library

cardMetrics.label: OPEN SOURCE
cardMetrics.value: Driver library published to mbed repository

cardMetrics.label: DEPTH
cardMetrics.value: Register-level I2C driver from datasheet to working code

cardMetrics.label: SCOPE
cardMetrics.value: mbed LPC1768 driver + Pi Pico port attempted

costSavings.remoteDiagnostics: ~$3K per on-site visit reduced (visit frequency varies)
